This species is a type of lycopod, a group of plants that are often characterized by their simple, leaf-like structures and ability to thrive in shaded environments. Lycopodioides cathedrifolia is a member of the genus Lycopodioides, which is part of the larger phylum Lycopodiophyta. The classification of Lycopodioides cathedrifolia reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a primitive, non-vascular plant. Lycopodioides cathedrifolia's classification is often used in botanical research to study the evolution and diversity of ancient plant lineages.

Lycopodium clavatum, also known as Wolf's Claw or Club Moss, is a perennial, evergreen plant that belongs to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae). The health benefits of Lycopodium clavatum include its use as a natural expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
